title: Out-of-bounds notebook replace errors instead of promoting to insert (CC parity gap)
description:
## Severity
Minor

## Dimension
Functional Correctness / Cognitive Readability

## Location
src/tools/file/notebook.rs:173-181 (replace branch out-of-bounds check)

## Violation
```rust
if index >= cells.len() {
    return (format!("Cell {target_desc} is out of bounds. Notebook has {} cells ..."), true);
}
```

When the requested index equals cells.len() (i.e. one past the end), CC silently promotes the replace to an insert. OC errors out. This isn't necessarily wrong — but the test `notebook_replace_out_of_bounds_returns_error_not_insert` pins it as a deliberate parity gap. Either the gap must be closed, or the semantic decision documented in code comments alongside the error message so future maintainers do not "fix" it without intent.

## Mandated refactor
Decide explicitly: either (a) promote `replace` at `index == cells.len()` to `insert` (mirror CC) AND require `cell_type`, or (b) add an `///` doc comment to the function and a comment inline at the bounds check stating "OC intentionally rejects index == cells.len(); CC promotes silently — divergence is intentional, see issue #XXX." Without one of these two, the divergence is undiscoverable from source.

## Filed from QA review dispatch
